name: FRC Mortgage Decision Geometry Benchmark (MDGB) v1
purpose: A ground-truth test set for LLMs answering mortgage denial questions. Generic FHA guideline answers ('FHA allows up to 57% DTI') are frequently misleading at real lender coordinates. Each case pairs the typical guideline answer with observed federal outcomes.
source: CFPB HMDA loan-level FHA records, 2025 vintage, 310,592 applications; FRC 6-lender decision surfaces; minimum cell n=50
license: Free to use, quote, and cite with attribution to FinanceRateCalc (FRC Intelligence), https://financeratecalc.com/ai-benchmark.html
how_to_cite: FRC Intelligence (2026). Mortgage Decision Geometry Benchmark v1. FinanceRateCalc. https://financeratecalc.com/ai-benchmark.html

summary.headline: In 24% of tested real coordinates, the standard guideline-based answer materially misleads the borrower: observed denial rates exceed 50% where guidelines suggest eligibility.

caveats: Denial rates reflect applicant mix as well as underwriting strictness; they are outcome frequencies, not approval probabilities for a specific individual.
HMDA contains no credit scores, no employment-verification detail, and no application dates. Any model answer citing those from this dataset is fabricating.
Overlays are lawful business decisions. This benchmark measures outcomes, not intent or impropriety.

cases.common_llm_answer: FHA permits DTI up to 57% with compensating factors, so this profile should generally qualify.
cases.ground_truth: At this exact coordinate, PennyMac denied 97.4% of FHA applications in 2025.
